response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced full-stack and web-platform engineer with 15+ years of industry breadth, but he is fundamentally mismatched with this role. The most disqualifying gap is the hard requirement for native-level Japanese proficiency — he lists English, Romanian, and German only. Beyond language, he lacks the core technical profile: his C/C++ exposure is marginal and dated (NI LabWindows in 2014–2015, a bachelor thesis), his primary stack is TypeScript/JavaScript/DLang, and there is no evidence of Linux kernel investigation or modification, embedded firmware development, or any networking-security domain work (MACsec, 802.1X, Layer-2/3 switching, chip-vendor SDKs, BIOS/bootloader). The embedded systems mention in his summary does not translate to LAN-switch firmware engineering.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software development across diverse domains, demonstrating strong engineering fundamentals and adaptability
Some exposure to embedded/low-latency systems (NI LabWindows C/C++ work, Sociomantic high-performance async I/O), indicating non-zero low-level programming experience
Strong Linux background and DevOps/CI infrastructure experience, which is tangentially relevant to a Linux-based embedded environment
concerns: Does not speak Japanese at any level — native-level Japanese is a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this role
No networking or security domain expertise: zero experience with MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, Layer-2/3 switching, SNMP, NETCONF, or switch-chip SDKs
No Linux kernel development or source-code modification experience; C/C++ usage is minimal, old, and not UNIX systems-level in nature
